methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Biobot Analytics is a Series B biotech & health company founded by Mariana Matus and Newsha Ghaeli that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 8 private peers in the same sector and stage, plus 3 public biotech & health companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) for reference.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $40M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implying a $76M valuation. Peer-median analysis draws from four same-sector, same-stage comparables—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yielding a median valuation of $1.3B and a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; while the sector median trades at 6.1x price-to-sales, Biobot's revenue is not disclosed, preventing meaningful P/S application.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $65M (low) to $1.5B (high) with a median of $688M. The significant spread reflects material uncertainty around Biobot's growth trajectory and market penetration relative to the peer set, which exhibits high valuation dispersion. The stage-progression method anchors conservatively at $76M, while peer-median positioning at $1.3B assumes performance closer to upper-quartile comparables. Without disclosed revenue or unit economics, revenue-based methods remain unavailable, limiting precision in this early-stage assessment.
